summary.rationale: The final score reproduces the user-selected Scheherazade comparison direction as one coherent type family. Compact baseline qualities remain legible, true alterations keep their hierarchy, and dense mobile rows fit without collisions.

notes: The rejected Edwin treatment is absent from rendered document chords and served component CSS.
All required mobile and desktop browser evidence is present.
Representative rows contain no redundant labels, horizontal overflow, or detected chord collisions.
Roots and suffixes read as a unified scan unit while the 72% quality scale reduces crowding.
Inline root and bass accidentals stay attached to their note names, while true alterations remain distinctly raised.
The result directly follows the comparison option selected by the user.
The Scheherazade triangle and minus have restrained, compatible proportions beside the letterforms and extension figures.
The notation remains grounded in the score surface and the established jazz shorthand hierarchy.
One chord type family avoids the mixed-register mismatch rejected in earlier refinements.
